rationale.R1.2: The section contains a severe load-bearing logical error: the aggregate miscalibration gap widens from 0.13 (|0.74−0.61|) to 0.21 (|0.86−0.65|) under SelfCheck, yet the text interprets joint upward movement of confidence and accuracy as evidence of improved calibration. Additionally, 3 of 5 per-category gaps worsen while the text dismisses all three as noise. Logical consistency is a plausible primary responsibility if the user wants a substantive review.
rationale.R1.3: The headline claim 'uniformly reduces overconfidence' is directly falsified by the reported numbers: code-execution (0.14→0.24), file-read (0.11→0.19), and database-query (0.18→0.26) all show larger gaps under SelfCheck. Evidence-claim alignment is likely central regardless of whether the requested help is a review or an edit.
rationale.R1.6: 'Help me with this section' commonly signals a request for prose-level assistance; writing polish is a plausible load-bearing interpretation independent of the evidential problems, making it co-equal with R1.2 and R1.3 until clarified.

parsed.clarification_question: Do you want (a) a logical and evidence-integrity review checking whether the data actually support the claims, (b) prose and writing polish only, or (c) both?
